General safety information
Important safety instructions
1.1 Important safety instructions
It is important to install, operate, maintain, and dispose of the equipment according to the
information in the product documentation, and in compliance with local regulations. For the
safety of the users and the patients, before a user operates or works on the equipment, they
must read, understand, and obey all:
• Warnings, cautions, instructions, and notes in this document and the related
documents.
• Safety instructions, emergency procedures, and information in this document and the
related documents.
• Warnings, cautions, and safety markings on the equipment and the accessories of the
equipment.
If you have local work instructions for the equipment, then Elekta recommends that you include
the warnings, cautions, and notes from this document in your local work instructions.
1.1.1 Conventions for warnings, cautions, and notes
The conventions give information about the class and the level of the risk in the warnings and
cautions, and the content of notes.
WARNING 1.1
A warning is information about procedures, tasks, or conditions with a risk of fatal or serious
injury, injury, or clinical mistreatment, if you do not obey the instructions.
CAUTION 1.1
A caution is information about procedures, tasks, or conditions with a risk of damage to the
equipment, data, or the environment, if you do not obey the instructions.
Note: A note gives more information about a related text.
1.1.2 Expected service life of the equipment
The expected service life is the length of time that the regular service and repair procedures can
keep the equipment safe to operate.
WARNING 1.2
Do not operate the equipment unless you correctly complete the safety checks, planned
maintenance schedules, and extended life checks. If you ignore this warning, you can cause
fatal injury and damage to the equipment.
The expected service life of this equipment is 15 years of normal use.
The Elekta definition for 'expected service life' is the length of time that the regular service and
repair procedures can keep the equipment safe to operate.
The Elekta definition of 'normal use' is the treatment of 50 patients each day for five days each
week of the year. If the equipment is used more frequently than this, the frequency of the
planned maintenance must increase accordingly and the extended life checks must also be
brought forward accordingly.

Elekta Medical Linear Accelerator Document ID: 1014603_11

General safety information
Important safety instructions
The level of operation of the equipment can be different to ‘normal use’. This has an effect on the
length of the service life. Because of wear of parts, the risk of a malfunction can increase after 10
years operation. Elekta recommends that before you continue operation after 10 years, you
complete the regular extended life checks.
Elekta can help the User with the correct safety checks, planned maintenance schedules, and the
regular extended life checks for continued safe operation of the equipment.
The system must be maintained throughout its expected service life in compliance with the EMC
information detailed in the applicable Instructions for Use.
For information on the above checks and schedules, contact your local Elekta representative.

of, and work on, the equipment.

1.2 Safety precautions
1.2.1 Safety precautions for the compatibility of the equipment
WARNING 1.3
Do not use parts, accessories, or other equipment, without approval by Elekta of their
compatibility. If you ignore this warning, you can cause fatal injury and damage to the
equipment.
Only operate the equipment with Elekta supplied or approved, compatible equipment or parts.
Accessories, transducers, and cables that are not specified by or do not have the approval of
Elekta can cause an incorrect treatment delivery. They can also have an effect on the performance
of the electromagnetic compatibility (EMC), which can increase the emissions or decrease the
immunity of the equipment.
Contact Elekta for information about the compatibility of other equipment or parts.
1.2.2 Safety precautions for the electromagnetic compatibility
The design and manufacture of Elekta products is in compliance with applicable EMC standards.
The intended result of the standards is to minimize the risk of electromagnetic interference by RF
devices. The type of device and the distance to the equipment has an effect on the intensity of
the electromagnetic interference. The equipment includes the linear accelerator and accessories,
the control cabinets, the computers in the control room, and equipment from other
manufacturer.
Not controlled when downloaded or printed
Document ID: 1014603_11 Elekta Medical Linear Accelerator
Agility™ Corrective Maintenance Manual
Page 10 © Elekta Group 2022. All rights reserved.

General safety information
Safety precautions
It is necessary to have special precautions for EMC emissions. Elekta recommends that you obey
these precautions when you use RF devices near the equipment.
• Do not use RF devices unless they are in compliance with international standards for
electromagnetic compatibility, for example, the CE or FCC mark.
• Do not use a two-way emergency radio in the treatment room or the control room.
Only use an emergency radio if there is an emergency.
• Do not use cellular telephones, or other devices with cellular modems near the
equipment. The Instructions for Use contains information on the minimum safe
distance of these devices from the equipment.
• When you use a device that is enabled for wireless operation, it must be a minimum of
10 cm from the equipment.
• Radio-Frequency Identification (RFID) readers, when activated to do a scan of a RFID
tag, must be a minimum of one meter from the equipment.
Note: The RFID systems that Elekta supply use lower power levels. You can safely use these RFID systems
when you obey the procedures in the Instructions for Use for the product.
The system must be installed, put into service, and maintained in compliance with the EMC
information in the Instructions for Use.
Contact Elekta for information about the compatibility of parts and equipment.
WARNING 1.4
Do not put portable or mobile radio frequency (RF) communication devices near to the
equipment. The emissions from such RF devices can be more than the EMC standards, and can
have unwanted effects on the equipment operation. If you ignore this warning, the RF
emissions can cause the equipment to have a malfunction. A malfunction can cause fatal injury,
clinical mistreatment, or damage to the equipment.
WARNING 1.5
Do not use accessories, transducers, and cables that are not specified by Elekta. They can have
an effect on the EMC performance, which can increase EMC emissions or decrease the
immunity to RF interference of the equipment. If you ignore this warning, incorrect radiation
delivery and other malfunctions can occur, which can cause fatal injury or clinical mistreatment.
1.2.3 Safety precaution for Service Tools and lifting equipment
WARNING 1.6
Do not use defective or damaged lifting equipment. If you ignore this warning, you can cause
fatal injury and damage to the equipment.
WARNING 1.7
Do not use incorrectly calibrated or defective Service Tools. If you ignore this warning, you can
cause injury and damage to the equipment.
Before you use the Service Tool or lifting equipment, make sure that:
• There are no defective or damaged parts
• The calibration of the Service Tool is correct

General safety information
Safety precautions
• The applicable calibration certificate is valid
• The lifting equipment gives the maximum Safe Working Load (SWL).
For information on the maintenance and calibration schedules of the Service Tool, see the
Instructions for Use and Calibration Certificate for the tool.
1.2.4 Safety precaution for work with electrical and mechanical parts
WARNING 1.8
Do not connect any electrical power supply before the ground earth connections are secure.
There is a risk of high leakage current. If you ignore this warning, you can cause fatal injury.
WARNING 1.9
Do not remove the cables or the covers, or operate the equipment without the covers. If you
ignore this warning, you can cause fatal injury
The covers on the equipment prevent access to high voltage parts and parts that move. If you
remove the covers, there is a risk of high voltage shock or injury from parts that move.
Only remove the covers if you are an Elekta approved person with the correct authority and you
obey the applicable safety procedures.
1.2.5 Safety precaution for work with safety devices during service
operation
WARNING 1.10
Do not remove, change, or override the safety switches, interlocks, and other safety devices. If
you ignore this warning, you can cause fatal injury or clinical mistreatment.
The procedures in this document can tell you to remove, change, or override the safety switches,
interlocks, or other safety devices during repair or maintenance work. When the work is
completed, make all the safety devices active again, and do a test of them.
1.2.6 Safety precaution for work on BLDs with UV lighting
WARNING 1.11
Do not look into the ultraviolet lighting of the BLD without protective glasses. If you ignore this
warning, you can cause serious injury.
The covers and screens of the BLD prevent an injury to your eyes from the ultraviolet lighting.
Before you remove the covers or screens of the BLD, switch off the linear accelerator, or use
protective glasses. Protective glasses must have the UV-A specification OD 6+ @ 190 - 532 Nm.
If there is damage to the covers or screen of the BLD, switch off the linear accelerator and contact
the Elekta representative.

General safety information
Safety precautions
1.2.7 Safety precaution for work with lasers
WARNING 1.12
Do not look into the laser beams because they can cause damage to your eyes. If you ignore
this warning, you can cause serious injury.
Elekta systems use the laser beams to help set up patients in the correct position for treatment.
During your work in the treatment room, make sure that you do not look into the laser beams.
1.2.8 Safety precautions for work with display screen equipment
WARNING 1.13
Do not use the display screen equipment for long periods of time. If you ignore this warning,
you can cause injury.
When you use the display screen equipment (DSE), obey the applicable Health and Safety
regulations and procedures in the jurisdiction where you do the work.
1.2.9 Safety precautions for cleaning and disinfection
WARNING 1.14
Do not clean the equipment, or use a disinfectant spray when the equipment is connected to
the electrical supply. If you ignore this warning, you can cause fatal injury.
WARNING 1.15
Do not use a spray that is explosive or flammable. If you ignore this warning, you can cause
fatal injury and damage to the equipment.
CAUTION 1.2
Do not let water and other liquids go into the equipment. If you ignore this caution, you can
cause damage to the equipment.
CAUTION 1.3
Do not use a spray to clean the medical equipment room. If you ignore this caution, you can
cause damage to the equipment.
At regular intervals, it is necessary to clean the equipment and equipment room with
disinfectants. Elekta recommends that the procedures to clean the equipment and the equipment
room obey the applicable laws and regulations of the jurisdiction where the equipment is
installed.

General safety information
Safety precautions
1.2.10 Safety precautions for work with high temperatures on the BLD
covers
During normal operation, the maximum temperatures of the accessory ring and BLD outer covers
can increase to 45 °C. Make sure that you do not touch the outer covers or the accessory ring for
more than 1 minute.
1.2.11 Safety precaution for the installation of accessories on the BLD
WARNING 1.16
Do not touch the connector found in the accessory ring on the BLD. If you ignore this warning,
you can cause injury.
When you install an accessory, for example, a shadow tray, electron applicator, or front pointer,
do not touch the pins in the connector. The pins have a low electrical current, which can give an
electric shock when you touch them.
1.2.12 Safety precautions for work with heavy parts
The safety precautions for work with heavy parts are the necessary safety precautions to know to
prevent injury.
WARNING 1.17
Do not lift or move heavy parts unless you obey the applicable Health and Safety regulations
and procedures. If you ignore this warning, you can cause serious injury.
1.2.13 Safety precautions during work on the BLD
When you do work on the BLD, make sure that you obey the following safety precautions:
WARNING 1.18
Do not do work on the inner parts of the beam limiting device while the electrical supply is
connected. There is a risk of high voltage electric shock. If you ignore this warning, you can
cause fatal injury.
WARNING 1.19
Do not do work on the beam limiting device unless the gantry is at 0° or 180°. Gravity can cause
the leaves, diaphragms, and dynamic leaf guides to move. If you ignore this warning, you can
cause fatal injury.
WARNING 1.20
Do not touch the hot LED field light projector, or the heat sink, immediately after operation. If
you ignore this warning, you can cause serious injury.

General safety information
Safety precautions
1.2.14 Safety precaution for the removal of parts from the radiation head
WARNING 1.21
Do not touch the flight tube, bending magnet, or other parts near to them until a check for
induced radioactivity is completed. Excessive exposure to radioactivity is dangerous. If you
ignore this warning, you can cause serious injury.
After the operation of the linear accelerator at high energy, the parts in the radiation head can
become radioactive and a radiation hazard. Before you do work on, or remove parts from the
radiation head, use a calibrated radiation detector to measure the level of induced radioactivity.
Use the applicable safety procedures when you remove radioactive parts and material. The
maximum dose rate for the safe removal of radioactive parts and material is 50 µSv/hr
(microsievert per hour). Do not remove the radioactive parts and material from the treatment
room before they are correctly prepared for safe removal.
1.2.15 Safety precautions for maintenance, modification, and
decommissioning
WARNING 1.22
Do not do work, or make modifications to the equipment without the correct training and
authority. If you ignore this warning, you can cause fatal injury, clinical mistreatment, and
damage to the equipment.
Only do work on, or make modifications to, the equipment if you are an Elekta approved person
with the applicable authority. Such work must use best practice and obey the laws and
regulations in the jurisdiction in which the equipment is installed.
Modifications or work that does not have approval by Elekta, and is not done by an Elekta
approved person with the correct authority, can cause:
• Fatal injury
• Incorrect radiation delivery
• Malfunction of the equipment
• Damage to the equipment
• The warranty to be canceled.
1.2.16 Safety precautions for work in areas of induced radioactivity
The safety precautions for work in areas of induced radioactivity are the precautions to prevent
an excessive exposure to, and serious injury from, induced radioactivity.
When you operate or do work on the equipment, Elekta recommends that you use the applicable
radiation protection functions, devices, systems, procedures, and accessories. Always obey the
applicable laws and regulations for induced radioactivity for the jurisdiction in which the
equipment is installed.
The safety precautions are applicable to:
• Maintenance, modifications, and decommissioning
• Measurement of the induced radioactivity
• Removal and movement of parts with induced radioactivity.
Not controlled when downloaded or printed
Elekta Medical Linear Accelerator Document ID: 1014603_11
Agility™ Corrective Maintenance Manual
© Elekta Group 2022. All rights reserved. Page 15

General safety information
Safety precautions
1.2.16.1 Safety precautions for the measurement of induced radioactivity
The safety precautions for the measurement of induced radioactivity are the necessary safety
precautions to know to prevent excessive exposure to radioactivity, which can cause serious
injury or damage to the environment.
Always use two radiation detectors to measure the level of induced radioactivity. Only use
radiation detectors that have an applicable calibration certificate. The period of time since the
last calibration date must be less than 12 months. Do not use the radiation detectors if the period
of time since the last calibration date is more than 12 months.
WARNING 1.23
Do not move radioactive material (by air, sea, or land) unless you obey the applicable laws and
regulations for induced radioactivity and disposal of radioactive material of the jurisdictions
through which the movement occurs. If you ignore this warning, you can cause serious injury
and damage to the environment.
1.2.16.2 Safety precautions for handling parts with induced radioactivity
The handling of parts with induced radioactivity must comply with the laws and regulations of the
jurisdiction in which the equipment is installed. The safe levels of radiation applicable in the UK
do not apply in all jurisdictions. The permitted level of radioactivity must always be as low as
reasonably achievable (ALARA). This level may vary depending on local rules, therefore the safe
working levels from the applicable local rules must be considered as the acceptable working limit.
If there are no safe levels available for a jurisdiction, use the UK value as a guideline, which is a
maximum of 150 cps at the surface for a maximum duration of 72 minutes. The following table
shows the conversion from this value to other units.
Table 1.2 Conversion between the units of induced radioactivity
Counts per second Microsieverts per Milliroentgen per
(cps) hour (µSv/hr) hour (mR/hr)
150 50 5
For information on the safe removal of parts with induced radioactivity and the safe levels of
radiation for your jurisdiction, contact Elekta or a Qualified Expert (QE).
1.3 Emergencies
1.3.1 Safety precaution for the occurrence of fire or explosion
WARNING 1.24
Do not use the equipment in rooms that contain explosive or flammable gases. If you ignore
this warning, you can cause fatal injury and damage to the equipment.
High levels of oxygen, and some anesthetic gases and disinfectant sprays, can make an explosive
or flammable environment in the room.
Not controlled when downloaded or printed
Document ID: 1014603_11 Elekta Medical Linear Accelerator
Agility™ Corrective Maintenance Manual
Page 16 © Elekta Group 2022. All rights reserved.

General safety information
Emergencies
1.3.2 Recommendations for emergency procedures
Before you use the equipment, Elekta recommends that you know all the local emergency
procedures and the locations of all the emergency switches.
The Elekta equipment includes:
• The STOP MOTORS buttons on the handheld controllers and user interface panels in
the treatment room, and FKP in the control room.
When you press these buttons, it disconnects the electrical supply from the motors,
stops all movements of the equipment, and stops kV and MV radiation.
• The EMERGENCY POWER OFF buttons in the treatment room, control area, and areas
near the equipment.
When you operate these buttons, it disconnects the electrical supply to the linear
accelerator. The buttons disconnect the electrical supply from many electrical circuits,
but not from all the circuits.

2.1.2 Recommendations for training
The regulations for training give a description of the necessary training, regulations, and
compliance that apply to the operation of the equipment.
Different countries have different regulations for training. Make sure that you have the necessary
training before you operate or do work on the equipment. Make sure that your training is in
compliance with the laws and regulations of the jurisdiction in which the equipment is installed.
Training is available from Elekta. Contact Elekta for more information about the applicable
training courses.
2.1.3 Disposal of the equipment
End of life (EOL) disposal is the procedure to disassemble, remove, and discard the equipment, in
full or in parts, to a condition where it is not possible to operate the equipment for its intended
use.
Only a Qualified Expert (QE) and an approved facility with an applicable license, may remove and
discard the equipment and recycle the material.
A full description of disposal requirements is detailed in a separate document Product Disposal
Information. This document is available on request from Elekta.
The customer owns the data on the storage media. If there are any data storage media such as
HDDs, remove them from the device and hand them to the customer for disposal. If the customer
entrusts the disposal team to dispose of the data, the data must be destroyed in accordance with
relevant cybersecurity regulations.

2.3.1 Precautions for work with electrostatic discharge sensitive devices

Regulatory information
Regulatory requirements for equipment use
CAUTION 2.2
Do not touch the parts identified with the ESD caution label. Always obey the ESD precautions
during work on these parts. If you ignore this caution, you can cause damage to the equipment.
Semiconductor devices, integrated circuits, and the parts that contain them, are ESD sensitive
devices. These devices are electrostatic discharge sensitive (ESDS) devices. ESD can cause damage
to these devices and cause them to have a malfunction immediately or some time after the
electrostatic discharge. To prevent the ESD damage to these devices, always wear a grounded
antistatic wrist strap, and obey the applicable ESD precautions and procedures.
Elekta recommends that all users receive the training about ESD. The training must include the
ESD caution label and these ESD precautions.

Introduction
Document scope
3.1 Document scope
The corrective maintenance manual (CMM) helps service engineers troubleshoot, repair and
maintain the systems, subsystems and components of the linear accelerator. The target audience
is qualified service engineers. These may be engineers employed directly by Elekta, qualified
representatives of Elekta or qualified representatives of Elekta's customers.
The manual provides:
• A description of the system or sub-system in question.
• A troubleshooting section to identify the problem areas.
• A set of detailed procedures used to replace FRUs (field replaceable units).
• A section on how to return the device to work.
If you find any mistakes in this document, or identify areas for improvement, please raise a CLM
(customer lifecycle management) case and assign it to the relevant queue.

Glossary of terms
3.3 Glossary of terms
Table 3.3 Glossary of terms
Term Definition
Autotrack function The autotrack function automatically moves the diaphragms or
leaves to their prescribed position.
Cal block The Cal block contains the item and part values that, when
loaded, configure the linear accelerator for a given energy.
Clinical Mode In Clinical Mode, Clinical Users use the linear accelerator and its
accessories for the radiation therapy treatment that a licensed
medical practitioner prescribes.
Gating Gating is the procedure by which an external system or manual
task pauses and starts the radiation delivery as a result of
information received relating to the treatment position of the
patient.
Interdigitation Interdigitation is an overlap of the opposite adjacent leaves of a
multileaf collimator.
Service life Service life is the length of time that the regular servicing and
repair procedures can keep the machine in operation.
Service Mode In Service Mode, Service Users do the necessary maintenance
tasks and calibration of the equipment.

4.1.3 Integrity™ R4.x treatment control system (TCS)
Integrity™ R4.x provides the user interface for the treatment control system. It is part of the
consolidated computing platform (CCP) and runs as a virtual machine on the Enterprise Server.
The Enterprise Server is installed in the treatment control cabinet (TCC). The TCC is normally
located in either the treatment suite control room or a server room.
Refer to the EMLA Service User Guide for: Integrity with the Consolidated Computing Platform
(CCP) for information on fault finding for Integrity™ R4.x and the TCC.
The linear accelerator has two modes of operation, delivering either an X-ray beam or an electron
beam. For each mode, the beam shape is changed through the BLD. For a full description, refer to
the EMLA Instructions for Use Volume 1 manual.

Troubleshooting
Tools and test equipment
5.1 Tools and test equipment
The instructions in this chapter assume that the attending service engineer has an Elekta standard
engineers toolkit. Any special tools, test equipment or consumable items necessary to to a
specific procedure are listed with the procedure.
5.2 Identifying a leaf drive motor
Each LMD motherboard controls 40 leaf drive motors.
Each motherboard has a label. The twenty outer leaf motors are at the bottom of the label, the
twenty inner leaf motors are at the top.
The Y1 Target motherboard controls Y1 ODD leaves.

5.4 Switches on Agility™ PCBs
5.4.1 Locating the projector LED ON/OFF switch
This projector LED ON/OFF switch (1) (see Figure 5.13) is used to switch off the projector LEDs for
maintenance purposes. It is located on the LED PCB in the router PCB cage.
Not controlled when downloaded or printed

Agility™ Corrective Maintenance Manual

Troubleshooting workflows
2
1
NOT TO SCALE 005314 ©2013 Elekta Limited
Figure 5.13 LED rocker switch
The LED ON/OFF switch is useful in two ways:
• You can switch off the LEDs before working on the BLD to avoid any danger from
UV radiation. This is useful if you are removing the crosswire screen.
• The LED rocker switch does not power off the projector cooling fan, so it can be
used to cool down the projector. Power off the LEDs and wait at least five minutes
for the projector to cool down before you remove the projector for maintenance
work.

Service parts repair or replacement
Safety precautions for BLD maintenance
6.1 Safety precautions for BLD maintenance
6.1.1 Making the linear accelerator safe for Agility™ BLD maintenance
WARNING 6.1
Do not do work on the Agility™ beam limiting device unless the gantry is at 0° or 180°. Gravity
can cause the leaves, diaphragms and DLGs to move. If you ignore this warning, it can cause
fatal injury.
1 Rotate the gantry to 180°.
2 Rotate the Agility™ BLD to 0°.
This is necessary to remove the inner covers.
3 Set the treatment table at 0° and the column at 90°.
4 At the interface cabinet, switch off the circuit breaker CB7 (ICCA).
This will keep the Leaf Limits (40×40) shape.
WARNING 6.2
Do not remove the BLD unless you fit the gantry locking straps and rear support post. The
gantry can become unbalanced. If you ignore this warning, parts that move can cause fatal
injury.
WARNING 6.3
Do not lift the Agility™ BLD unless you use a suitable hoist and fit the shackles and slings
provided. If you ignore this warning, you can cause fatal injury.
5 If you remove the Agility™ BLD, do the steps that follow:
a Install the bolts to attach a locking bar at each end of the gantry drum to:.
‒ The center of the support wheel
‒ The attachment point on the outer surface of the gantry drum.
b At the gun end of the gantry arm, install the bolts to attach the rear support bar to:
‒ The support bracket
‒ The nut at the attachment point in the site floor.
6 Load MLC Park field pattern to park the MLC components.
7 Load Leaf Limits (40×40) to move the leafbank, DLGs and diaphragms to their maximum
travel.
8 Shut down the system from the radiotherapy workstation.
Shutting down the system automatically parks the MLC.
WARNING 6.4
Do not do work on the inner parts of the beam limiting device when the power is on. The
power can cause the inner parts to move. If you ignore this warning, it can cause fatal injury.

WARNING 6.5
Do not remove the covers or cables from the equipment unless special instructions in this
document tell you to. The covers give access to high voltage electric parts. Always install the
covers and cables again before you use the equipment. If you ignore this warning, a high
voltage electric shock can cause fatal injury.
WARNING 6.6
Do not remove the covers from the equipment unless special instructions in this document tell
you to. The covers give access to parts that move. Always install the covers again before you
use the equipment. If you ignore this warning, parts that move can cause fatal injury.
WARNING 6.7
Do not look into the ultraviolet light without protective glasses when the Agility™ BLD covers or
crosswires screen are removed. If you ignore this warning, it can cause serious injury to your
eyes.
9 Remove the applicable covers to get access to the BLD.
10 For Elekta Harmony systems, do the steps that follow.
Note: This step is only applicable for Agility™ BLD with Harmony covers.
WARNING 6.8
Do not open the inner arm cover unless the gantry is at 180° ± 20°. Gravity can cause the cover
to move. If you ignore this warning, you can cause serious injury and damage to the equipment.
a Open the inner arm cover.
CAUTION 6.1
Always make sure that you can safely replace the applicable parts when the inner arm cover is
open. If not, you must remove the inner arm cover. If you ignore this caution, you can cause
damage to the equipment.
b Do a check of the inner parts of the BLD before replacement. Make sure that you can
safely remove the applicable part. If not, you must remove the inner arm cover.
6.1.2 Preparing the linear accelerator for operation after Agility™ BLD
maintenance
CAUTION 6.2
Make sure that you remove the locking bars and rear support bar from the gantry before you
start the gantry again. If you ignore this caution, it can cause damage to the equipment.
WARNING 6.9
Before you switch on the linear accelerator after BLD maintenance, make sure that all
assemblies and parts that were removed are installed correctly. If you ignore this warning,
serious injury and damage to the equipment can occur.
1 If you removed the Agility™ BLD, do the steps that follow:
a At the gun end of the gantry arm, remove the bolts and remove the rear support bar
from:

6.2 Covers
6.2.1 Removing the Agility™ BLD covers
WARNING 6.10
Do not do work on the inner parts of the beam limiting device while the electrical supply is
connected. There is a risk of high voltage electric shock. If you ignore this warning, you can
cause fatal injury.
WARNING 6.11
Do not remove the covers from the equipment unless special instructions in this document tell
you to. The covers give access to parts that move. Always install the covers again before you
use the equipment. If you ignore this warning, parts that move can cause fatal injury.
WARNING 6.12
Do not do work on the equipment after you disconnect the electrical supply before you do a
check for continued high voltage. Customer interfaces can continue to supply high voltage to
the electrical terminals. If you ignore this warning, you can cause fatal injury.
1 Remove the four M4 × 12 mm allen screws that hold the touchguard.
2 Lift away the touchguard.
3 Remove the four M4 × 12 mm allen screws that hold the covers to the BLD.
4 Hold one of the covers and pull it away from the BLD.
Note: A firm pull can be necessary to release the spring clips.

6.2.2 Installing the Agility™ BLD covers
CAUTION 6.3
Always make sure that the first cover is held in position before you attach the second cover. If
not, the first cover can become unattached and be damaged.
1 Attach the inner beam arm covers, the inner beam arm cover connecting screw, and the
ODI cover (refer to the EMLA Corrective Maintenance Manual for Covers set).
2 Hold one of the Agility™ BLD covers and put the top edge of the cover level with the
accessory ring.
a Push it into position around the Agility™ BLD and the touchguard actuator brackets.
b Make sure that the BLD cover spring clips have engaged with the cover bushes.
3 Do the process again for the second Agility™ BLD cover. Make sure that both covers are
installed securely.
4 Screw the covers to the BLD. Use the four M4 × 12 mm allen screws removed before.

6.7.3 Replacing the diaphragm brake
Prerequisites
Spares kit for the diaphragm brake, including the diaphragm brake and hub, brake fixing screws,
and woodruff key.
Tools:
• 1.5 mm hexagonal head key
• Bearing puller - With a 5 mm center pin and external gripping legs that fit the 30 mm
diameter of the collar of the split shaft clamp.
• Split shaft clamp - Suitable for a 12 mm shaft with a 30 mm external diameter and
12 mm internal diameter. It must be compatible with the bearing puller.
When you replace the diaphragm brake, you must also replace the brake hub. Any damage to the
edges of the hexagon on the hub can cause excessive wear and prevent the brake from operating
correctly.

Note: Only install a new woodruff key if it is missing.

the diaphragm intermediate PCB.

d Reconnect the cable linking the brake to the diaphragm intermediate PCB.
6 Make sure that the diaphragm brake operates correctly.
Note: Check the brake hub for runout. If the shaft of the brake hub is bent, it can shorten the life span of
the brake and shaft.
a Move the diaphragm to a mid point.
b Set the gantry at 90° and the BLD at 90°.
Note: If the brake is not operating correctly, the hub can be seen to slowly turn as the diaphragm moves
under gravity. The diaphragm will not move when driven if the brake is not disengaging correctly.
7 Prepare the linear accelerator for operation.

6.10.4 Replacing a leaf ruby
Replacement leaf rubies are supplied attached to a ruby application tool. The ruby application
tool is supplied as part of a spares kit, including also the leaf clamp, scalpel, adhesive and paint
marker pen.
1 Make sure that the linear accelerator is safe for Agility™ BLD maintenance.
2 Identify the defective leaf.
3 If you replace a leaf ruby in an isocenter LMD module, remove the LMD PCBs.
4 Remove the LMD module related to the leaf to be repaired.
5 Remove the defective leaf.
6 Carefully scrape off any old glue residue from the leaf ruby recess.
7 Mix together a small amount of the two part adhesive and apply a small amount to the cup
where the ruby mounts.
8 Gently press the new ruby into the adhesive.
Note: Replacement rubies are supplied in a ruby applicator tool (4). This can be used to slide the ruby
into place on the leaf, then the tool can be removed once the adhesive has set. Alternatively, the
ruby can be removed from the tool before being glued to the leaf.
9 Attach the leaf clamp tool (see Figure 6.125) to clamp the ruby in place and leave for half an
hour for the adhesive to set.

Service parts repair or replacement
DLG linear slides replacement
6.12 DLG linear slides replacement
6.12.1 Doing the DLG linear slides replacement
This task gives the full workflow in the sequence that is necessary to replace the DLG linear
slides.
1 In addition to steps 2 to 8 , agree with the customer representative what checks must be
done both before the replacement and return to work checks.
2 Do the check Examining the DLG drive current (Agility™).
Refer to the latest version of the EMLA Planned Maintenance Information manual.
3 Do the Leaf and DLG drive backlash checks.
This is a reference before you start the replacement work.
4 Do a backup of the linac calibration files.
5 Do the optical system calibration without the radiation program.
a Make sure that the optical system calibration passes, but do not save.
b Record the results.
c If the optical system calibration does not pass and the BLD moves usually, correct the
problem.
6 Do the Agility Diagnostic check. If the fan speed test fails, continue with the check.
Note: This is a known issue. If you think this can cause a failure, contact your local Elekta representative.
7 On the Mechanism page, set Item 319: target cntrl to 1. Do not save.
8 Do the procedure Preparing the hardware for Agility™ BLD maintenance.
Related Links:
About this return to work checks section on page 306
6.12.2 Preparing the hardware for Agility™ BLD maintenance
WARNING 6.38
Do not do work on the Agility™ beam limiting device unless the gantry is at 0° or 180°. Gravity

travel.

6.12.12 Doing a check of the correct alignment of the leaf bank assembly
WARNING 6.54
Do not use the equipment unless you completed a check of the correct alignment of the leaf
bank assembly. The result can be incorrect radiation delivery and other malfunctions. If you
ignore this warning, it can cause fatal injury and clinical mistreatment.
1 Select one leaf bank (leaf bank 1).
2 Move all the leaves to the outer limit.
3 Move the two DLGs fully out to the end-stop.
This creates an open field.
4 On leaf bank 1, move the DLG fully forward to the end-stop.
5 Move the DLG for leaf bank 1 approximately three mm away from the end-stop.
6 Move leaves on leaf bank 1 fully forward to their end-stop.
7 Use a feeler gauge to measure the clearance between the outer leaves and the fixed side
diaphragm on each side of the leaf bank (see Figure 6.162).
0012_LAGI
Figure 6.162 Measuring the clearance
a Make sure that the clearance is consistent along the length of the interface between
the outer leaf on each side of the leaf bank 1 and the Fixed Side Diaphragm.
b Move the feeler gauge smoothly forwards and backwards between each outer leaf
and the Fixed Side Diaphragm on both sides of leaf bank 1 across the full over-lapping
range.
Note: A feeler gauge of different thickness can be necessary for each side.
Note: The leaves are put together in a block to prevent the movement of the outer leaves, which can
give an incorrect measurement of the clearance.

012883_001
Figure 7.7 Log File Display tab of the Diagnostic Utility
Log file Sorting
This lets you put the log files in for machine state changes in sequence (for example, suspends,
interrupts, and terminated treatments). This lets you find a problem more easily.
Date Filter
It is possible to set a filter to show only the log files that were saved between two dates. Be
careful when you type the date. Elekta recommends that you use a three letter abbreviation for
the month, for example, ‘10 Nov 11’. Also, Elekta recommends that you set filters for dates,
because some time is necessary for 999 log files to load (the maximum available).
Other Information
You can look at the calibration files, energy calibration files, linac terminations, and HT Hours/Life
Dose. The energy calibration files show the saved values for a given energy. The calibration files
do not give much information for the field engineer. The Linac Terminations filter shows all the
log files for suspends and terminated treatments. HT Hours/Life Dose gives information on the
dose delivered during the life of the machine.
Note: When there are 999 log files, some time is necessary before the system can show the terminated
treatments only.
MLC Log files
The MLC log files are saved with the log files for the linear accelerator. The Log File Format is
different to that of the linear accelerator.
Note: There are always entries in the log files with RECOV at the end. These show general errors that
cleared after two or three tries.

Setting up, testing and calibrating Agility™
Agility™ MLC drive checks
7.2 Agility™ MLC drive checks
The drive checks are used to find a fault in the drive systems.
7.2.1 Doing the leaf drive current check
The maximum leaf motor currents are measured while the leaves are moved in and out. First with
the gantry at 90° and the BLD at 0°, then with the BLD at 180°. This gives the motor currents
when the leaves move against gravity.
1 Make sure that Service Graphing is configured to allow the graphs to be saved.
2 Rotate the gantry to +90°.
3 Rotate the BLD to 0°.
4 In the Deliver Quick Beam window, set the Radiation Type and Energy.
5 Click on the MLC icon then click on the Display Shape Codes icon.
6 Load the Interdigitate Full Evens shape.
7 Monitor the image and make sure that all the leaves move to the correct position.
8 Click on the Service Graphing icon.
9 Select Files.
10 In the Manage configurations field, select Leaf 40 position v leaf motor currents.
11 Click Load.

7.3.1 About this return to work checks section
The checks in this section may be required after replacement of spare parts.
Note: Replacing individual leaves should not affect leaf calibration. However, removal of the wedge or
disturbing the optical components will affect calibration. Any changes to the position of the
reference reflectors will require a calibration check. In these circumstances, complete the Agility
MLC calibration.

7.3.2.2 Adjusting the camera focus
The lens aperture and focus are set when the lens is attached to the camera. If the camera and
lens is to be replaced as a single item, you do not need to adjust the lens focus. If only the lens is
to be replaced, you must do this procedure to do a check of the focus.
The Agility BLD uses ultraviolet light. This ultraviolet light is contained by the Agility BLD covers
and crosswires screen.

WARNING 7.1
Do not look into the ultraviolet light without protective glasses when the Agility BLD covers or
crosswires screen are removed. If you ignore this warning, the ultraviolet light can cause
serious injury to your eyes. Protective glasses must have UV-A specification OD 6+ @ 190 - 532
nm.
WARNING 7.2
Do not touch the LED field light projector and heatsink. They can become hot. Let it cool before
you start maintenance work. If you ignore this warning you can be burned.
1 Before you remove the covers or crosswires screen, turn off the ultraviolet light or use
protective glasses.
2 Withdraw the optics assembly a small amount from the BLD. Do not remove it completely
and do not disconnect the projector or camera.
3 Loosen the lens focus ring locking screw.
4 Adjust the focus slightly.
5 Install the optics assembly and do a check of the focus setting. Make sure that each leaf and
reference reflector is clearly shown. If it is not acceptable, repeat steps 1 to 3 .
6 When the lens focus is correct, tighten the lens focus locking screw.

reflector.

WARNING 7.5
Do not touch the LED field light projector and heatsink. They can become hot. Let it cool before

1 Rotate the gantry to 180°.
2 Rotate the Agility™ BLD to 90°.
3 Loosen all eight T10 Torx screws securing the crosswire screen to the BLD accessory ring.
4 Suspend a piece of translucent material (a piece of plain paper) over the center of the BLD.
Note: It must be suspended as near as possible to be BLD accessory ring, without it touching the BLD
accessory ring.
A gantry locking strap can be a suitable tool to suspend the material over the BLD. The
material must not rotate when the BLD is rotated.
5 If necessary, remove the touchguard actuators.
6 Make sure that the BLD can rotate through 360° without interference.
7 Use a sharp pencil to mark the center of the projected crosswires on the translucent
material.
8 Rotate the BLD through 180 degrees to -90°.
9 Record the error between the mark previously made and the new position of the projected
crosswire.
10 Loosen the knurled screws that hold the crosswire screen.
11 Adjust the position of the crosswire screen to halve the difference between the two
recorded positions, in the A-B and G-T axes.
12 Tighten the knurled screws that hold the crosswire screen.
13 Repeat until there is no discernible error.
14 Use Diagnostic Control to move the X1 diaphragm to within 1 mm of the crosswire center
field image.

7.4 Calibration
7.4.1 Calibrating the Agility™ BLD
The workflow performs the complete calibration of the Agility™ BLD. For more information see

Wedge checks
7.5.1 Adjusting the wedge potentiometers
1 Rotate the gantry to 180°.
2 Remove the BLD covers, if installed.
3 Do the procedure Make sure that the linear accelerator is safe for Agility™ BLD
maintenance, but do not power off the linear accelerator.
WARNING 7.10
Do not do work on the Agility™ beam limiting device unless the gantry is at 0° or 180°. Gravity
can cause the leaves, diaphragms and DLGs to move. If you ignore this warning, it can cause
fatal injury.
4 Rotate the BLD to ±90°.
5 Open the router PCB cage.
6 Disconnect the router PCB cage fan.
7 Remove the two 2.5 mm allen screws and remove the router PCB cage fan from router PCB
cage.
This allows access to wedge PCB potentiometers.
8 Load a standard X-ray beam (shutter in, wedge out).
9 Do the adjustment of the Wedge In/Out position from a centered position, not from a limit.
Note: A new wedge PCB can cause the wedge to drive into the limits if the potentiometer is set at limit,
which may cause thermal shutdown of the control chip. Thermal overload is indicated by the
orange LED near the comparator on the PCB illuminating. The LED turns off when the chip
temperature returns to within normal operating range. The wedge position is not determined by
the microswitches, it is determined by the potentiometer. Be aware, reset accordingly.
a Make sure that the Motor Enabled and Brake Disabled (energized) LEDs (orange) are
off.
Note: The motor delay is shorter than the brake delay.
b Adjust the Shutter In / Wedge Out potentiometer (middle potentiometer) one way
(clockwise or anti-clockwise) until either the shutter in or wedge out LED goes out.
Approximately 1/4 of a turn only are necessary for this adjustment.
c Turn the potentiometer the other way until the other LED goes out. Then set the
potentiometer to the mid-point between these two positions (see Figure 7.25).

7.6 Touchguard checks
7.6.1 Using the touchguard override links
It can be necessary during troubleshooting to override the touchguard actuators. To do this, the
touchguard override links must be temporarily attached to the accessory PCB.
1 Remove the BLD covers (if installed).
2 Remove the top plate of the accessory PCB cage to allow good access to the connectors.
Remove the two allen screws (1) that hold the cable form guide.
1 2
3
NOT TO SCALE 005313 ©2013 Elekta Limited
Figure 7.31 Agility™ touchguard actuators
Note: The touchguard override links are the free connectors in the cable form guide.
3 Disconnect SK26-E3 and SK26-E11 from the accessory PCB.
4 Connect the touchguard override links SK26-E3M and SK26-E11M to the accessory PCB.
5 After the troubleshooting, make sure that you remove the touchguard override links from
the accessory PCB.
6 Connect SK26-E3 and SK26-E11 to the accessory PCB.
7 Use the cable ties to attach the touchguard override link cables and connectors.
8 Install the top plate of the accessory PCB cage. Be careful that you do not damage the
accessory flexi circuit.

7.6.2 Adjusting the touchguard actuators
1 Make sure that the linear accelerator is in the Preparatory status.
2 Push the touchguard to activate the first touchguard actuator spring.
3 Make sure that inhibit message Touchguard appears.
4 Release the touchguard.
5 Make sure that the inhibit message Touchguard disappears.
6 Do steps 2 and 3 for each of the touchguard actuator springs.
7 Attach the touchguard to the touchguard actuator springs.
a Use the four M4/M5 Allen screws provided.
b Do not overtighten, as this can cause the Touchguard inhibit message to appear.
c Attach the touchguard.
Note: Make sure that the Agility™ BLD is installed in the correct orientation.
8 Make sure that the touchguard is parallel to the Agility™ BLD accessory ring.
9 Rotate the gantry to ±90°. Then rotate the BLD ±180°.
a Make sure that the Touchguard inhibit message does not appear.
b Make sure that the touchguard is not activated by gravity or movement.
10 Rotate the gantry ±180°.
a Make sure that the Touchguard inhibit message does not appear.
b Make sure that the touchguard is not activated by gravity or movement.
11 If necessary, adjust the touchguard actuators.
Note: You can adjust the touchguard microswitches in or out, and the microswitch bracket up or down.
12 Install the BLD covers if removed.

This procedure applies to installations of Agility™ and Integrity R4.x with a license for Volumetric
Intensity Modulated Arc Therapy (VMAT).

2 Click the Deliver Stored Beam button on the secondary function toolbar.
3 Select Fully Dynamic MLC160 from the Beams list.
4 Deliver the beam.
5 Monitor i2200 part 111.
Note: PRF Pauses must stay at zero.
6 Use the Dynamic Delivery Log File Viewer to make sure that there were no errors during
beam delivery.

8.12 Check DLG, diaphragm and leaf limits
These tests checks the current limit circuitry and potentiometer readings at outer end stops to
ensure required travel range could be achieved.
8.12.1 Test diaphragm limits at outer end stops
Set diaphragm motor current limit (700mA), 25% demands and direction: message 0x211 {-30,
60,-30, 60}. Send 1 byte diaphragm motor Enable message 0x210 {01 101 101}.
Wait for 20 seconds or until both diaphragms reach the end stops. Monitor messages 0x215 and
0x216 for coarse and check potentiometer readings until they stop changing by more than 30.
Then read message 0x213 for current limit of more than 20 ADC units.

the current reading. In the tables that follow the term stops is used if the change in value is less
than 30 ADC reading. The term moves is used if the change in value is more than 30 ADC reading.

8.14 DLG control tests
The DLG control tests compare the potentiometer values from the previous reading and the
current reading. In the tables that follow the term stops is used if the change in value is less than
30 ADC reading. The term moves is used if the change in value is more than 30 ADC reading.

Technical Reference
Environmental conditions for the transport, storage and operation of the Agility™ treatment head
9.3 Environmental conditions for the transport, storage and
operation of the Agility™ treatment head
Except where other allowable conditions are stated in the accompanying documents, general
environmental conditions for the treatment head during the transport, storage and operations
are as follows:
Table 9.1 Permitted environmental conditions for the transport and storage of the Agility™ treatment
head
Condition Permitted conditions
Ambient temperature -23°C to +70°C
Relative humidity 5% to 100%
Atmospheric pressure 50 kPa to 110 kPa
(500 mbar to 1100 mbar)
Vibration 4 g (10 Hz to 150 Hz)
Shock 25 g (duration 6 ms to 16 ms)
Note: The storage conditions for the Agility™ treatment head without its packaging are the same as the
environmental conditions for operation.
Table 9.2 Permitted environmental conditions for the operation of the Agility™ treatment head
Condition Permitted conditions
Ambient temperature +15°C to +35°C
Relative humidity (no condensation) 30% to 70%
Atmospheric pressure 70 kPa to 110 kPa
(700 mbar to 1100 mbar)
